correct_explanation: arithmetic dependency chains create long scoreboard stalls that prevent warp hiding
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: latency-bound
REASONING: The kernel achieves near-zero memory bandwidth and only 1% of peak FLOPS, yet occupancy is a reasonable 74.17%, ruling out occupancy as the primary issue. The dominant warp stall reason is "long scoreboard" at 57%, which indicates warps are waiting on long-latency operations (typically memory requests) to complete, but the negligible bandwidth utilization means the kernel is not saturating the memory subsystem—it is simply stalled waiting for high-latency dependent operations. This pattern is characteristic of a latency-bound kernel where there is insufficient instruction-level or thread-level parallelism to hide the latency of dependent memory or arithmetic operations.
parsed_reasoning: The kernel achieves near-zero memory bandwidth and only 1% of peak FLOPS, yet occupancy is a reasonable 74.17%, ruling out occupancy as the primary issue. The dominant warp stall reason is "long scoreboard" at 57%, which indicates warps are waiting on long-latency operations (typically memory requests) to complete, but the negligible bandwidth utilization means the kernel is not saturating the memory subsystem—it is simply stalled waiting for high-latency dependent operations. This pattern is characteristic of a latency-bound kernel where there is insufficient instruction-level or thread-level parallelism to hide the latency of dependent memory or arithmetic operations.
